citations.narrative:
The facility, which houses primates in outdoor enclosures, lacks an adequate perimeter fence. Several sections of the
fence are missing entire wooden planks, and gaps are present at the base due to wood deterioration. In addition, the
majority of the perimeter fence stands at less than 6 feet in height, and the distance between the west perimeter fence
and multiple primary enclosures measures only approximately 1 ft 4 inches.
An inadequate perimeter fence leaves the facility vulnerable and can allow intruders or wild animals to enter and
potentially endanger the primates.
The perimeter fence must prevent unauthorized humans and animals—such as dogs, skunks, and raccoons—from
entering or passing under it and contacting the nonhuman primates. It must be at least 6 feet high and positioned at least
3 feet from the primary enclosure to prevent any physical contact between animals inside the enclosure and those outside
the fence. Correct by June 1, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
n

narrative:
3.78(d) 
   
Outdoor housing facilities. 
The facility, which houses primates in outdoor enclosures, lacks an adequate perimeter fence. Several sections of the 
fence are missing entire wooden planks, and gaps are present at the base due to wood deterioration. In addition, the 
majority of the perimeter fence stands at less than 6 feet in height, and the distance between the west perimeter fence 
and multiple primary enclosures measures only approximately 1 ft 4 inches. 
 
An inadequate perimeter fence leaves the facility vulnerable and can allow intruders or wild animals to enter and 
potentially endanger the primates. 
 
The perimeter fence must prevent unauthorized humans and animals—such as dogs, skunks, and raccoons—from 
entering or passing under it and contacting the nonhuman primates. It must be at least 6 feet high and positioned at least 
3 feet from the primary enclosure to prevent any physical contact between animals inside the enclosure and those outside 
the fence. Correct by June 1, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
